summary: Quipu del Mani II -- TIER-2 REGIONAL AGGREGATOR of the Incahuasi peanut tribute accounting system (UE 11). 330 cords (289 L1 + 37 L2 + 4 L3) organized in 107 tiny clusters (60 triplets + 24 singletons + 10 doubles + 9 pentads) -- the most fragmented structure in the analysed corpus. Shares the nickname 'Quipu del Mani' with UR270 (UE 13). UNIQUE STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE: 9-cord EMPTY opening spacer (p1-p9), then ~200 cord-pairs of paired W (gross deposit) + NB (net after deduction) directly implementing Urton & Chu's (2015) deposit formula at the cord-pair level, closing with 14+13 empty cords at p251-264 and p277-289. 17 Ascher sums with deepest Merkle cascade (depth 4) of the Incahuasi set. 0/17 verified (same S-knot transcription issue as UR270/UR277). Benford 0.57 = standardized quota values (not raw tallies), confirming the aggregator role between UR270 (raw counts, Benford 0.83) and UR268 (district summary).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  KH0506 = UR269. Nickname (OKR): 'Quipu del Mani' (second khipu with this nickname alongside UR270).
  330 cords (289 L1 + 37 L2 + 4 L3). Architecture: 1-PAIRE depth L3.
  107 clusters: 60 triplets + 24 singletons + 10 doubles + 9 pentads + 2 quartets + 2 sextets. ZERO 9-cord clusters (distinguishes from UR278's 26 and UR270's 7).
  4 colors aggregated: NB(106), W(81), AB(64), MB(52). KFG reports 17 unique colors.
  Excavation: Sector A, Subsector 1, UA 4, Unidad 20N, UE 11.
  Museum: Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi, n. 13828.
  Cited in Urton & Chu 2015; Urton 2017; Medrano & Khosla 2024.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED=78, KFG_RESOLVED=110, OKR_ONLY=141, DIVERGENT=1.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.41.
  v1 -> v3: 2 -> 21 readable STRING (+19), 1 -> 9 vocabulary (+8), 110 L? filled.

ASCHER LAYER (diagnostic profile of an intermediate aggregator)
  17 pendant-pendant sums parsed (KFG catalog reports 43 total: 21 primary + 10 inter-group + 7 colored + 1 subsidiary).
  Max cascade depth = 4 -- DEEPEST Merkle tree of the four Incahuasi siblings (vs UR278 depth 2, UR277 depth 3, UR270 depth 3).
  covering_ratio = 0.16 -> LOW capacity (45/289 L1 involved). LOWEST coverage of the Incahuasi set.
  0/17 checksums verified (OKR S-knot transcription issue, same as UR270/UR277).
  Pass 3A/3C/3D: 0/0/0.
  Benford match = 0.5685 (LOW). UR269 = standardized quota values (2000s, 2300s, 2500s, 2700s round), not raw tallies. Consistent with aggregator role.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  9-cord EMPTY opening (p1-p9) -- unique in the analysed Incahuasi set.
  Heavy L1 cords at p14/p18/p19/p23/p24 (opening compound-records with 2 subsidiaries each).
  Paired W/NB body (p45-p250): ~200 cord-pairs of (W=gross, NB=net).
  Paired-W/NB deduction pattern (tax formula):
    p70 W=2700 / p71 NB=2600 (diff 100)
    p73 W=2810 / p74 NB=2700 (diff 110)
    p83 W=2460 / p84 NB=2418 (diff 42)
    p89 W=2633 / p90 NB=2500 (diff 133)
    p112 W=2000 / p113 NB=2000 (diff 0)
    ~200 more pairs -- the longest direct demonstration of Urton-Chu's formula in the corpus.
  Two long closing empty blocks: p251-264 (14 empties), p277-289 (13 empties).

VOCAB POSITIONS (chapter markers)
  p80, p102, p187, p235 = piqa (who/responsible) -- four anchors
  p106, p213, p215, p234 = taqa (boundary) -- four sub-regions
  p134, p153, p242 = chaqa (bridges/cuts)
  p140, p224 = qaqa (rocks)
  p180, p200 = waqa (huacas)
  p190 = chaqaqa (main bridge, topic-marked)
  p135, p232 = chiqa (verification points)
  p103, p240 = maqa (closing strikes)
  p141 = paqa (east orientation)
  Vocabulary threads through the body every 10-20 cords, indexing the provenance of each deposit cluster.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  NB (106) = NET / after-tax channel (paired).
  W (81) = GROSS / before-tax channel (paired).
  AB (64), MB (52) = structural markers + secondary data.
  The W/NB pairing is the document's PRIMARY ENCODING CHANNEL -- gross deposit recorded on W, net (after the Urton-Chu fixed tax) recorded on the adjacent NB cord. Every deposit processed through the aggregator receives two cords.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR269 is the intermediate tier of the three-tier peanut tribute pipeline at Incahuasi. It aggregates ~100 regional peanut deposits in paired W(gross)/NB(net) format implementing Urton-Chu's tax formula at the cord-pair level. The vocabulary indexes the PROVENANCE of each deposit (which locality, which landmark, which huaca, which bridge) without the ritual framing of UR270. The 4-level Merkle cascade and the 10 inter-group sums reported by KFG confirm a HIERARCHICAL AUDIT TRAIL structure, narrow-and-tall, consistent with a middle administrator who needs to verify tier-1 warehouse counts and carry them forward to tier-3 district totals.

THREE-TIER SYSTEM
  Tier 1: UR270 (UE 13) -- warehouse day-book with liturgical opening (pama/chama/taki), real counts, Benford 0.83.
  Tier 2: UR269 (UE 11) -- THIS KHIPU -- regional aggregator, paired W/NB tax formula, quota values, Benford 0.57, deepest Merkle cascade.
  Tier 3: UR268 (UE 18) -- district summary, 208-constant positional tax, presentation binding.

DOCUMENT TYPE: commodity_accounting (OVERRIDE of v3 classifier's cadastral_survey)
  The v3 classifier caught the 7/8 cadastral markers but missed the Urton-Chu tax-formula signature at cord-pair level and the nickname 'Quipu del Mani'. Keep commodity_accounting to preserve the three-tier framing and the nickname.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  The three-tier accounting architecture (day-book -> regional aggregator -> district summary) that Urton & Chu (2015) reconstruct for Incahuasi peanut tribute is most directly visible in UR269: the 200+ paired W/NB deposits apply the (gross - tax = net) formula one row at a time, over and over, across the document. The tax deductions vary by deposit (differences of 42, 100, 110, 133 -- not a constant fixed value), indicating the aggregator is reconciling VARIABLE local tax obligations rather than applying the flat 47 or 208 positional constants seen at the tier-3 summary. UR269 is the WORKBOOK of the pipeline: the middle register where the day-book's raw deliveries get converted into quota-compatible aggregates, one cord-pair at a time.
  The 9-cord empty opening may be a document-class header convention (a formal 'blank page' before a quota-aggregator's work begins) or a physical spacer (for rolling the khipu into its storage position).

KEY PASSAGES
  p1-p9: 9 EMPTY opening cords -- unique header convention.
  p70-p71 W=2700 / NB=2600: first documented gross-net pair (diff 100 = peanut tax on this deposit).
  p112-p113 W=2000 / NB=2000: diff 0 pair -- a deposit where no tax applies (likely a transfer within the system, not a new levy).
  p190 chaqaqa: topic-marked 'main bridge' -- central provenance anchor.
  p251-p264 (14 empties) and p277-p289 (13 empties): two long closing empty blocks -- end-of-document signature.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  ~200 paired W/NB cord-pairs: reproducible from v3 cord listing.
  Benford 0.57 for quota-round values: reproducible.
  Deepest Merkle cascade (depth 4): reproducible.
  Three-tier UR270 / UR269 / UR268 pipeline: Urton 2017 published framework.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - v3 classifier's cadastral_survey classification is overridden to commodity_accounting (tier-2 aggregator) because the cadastral markers are PROVENANCE INDEXES within a tribute register, not a land-survey document.
  - Previous confidence 'low' raised to high on structural signature evidence.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 330 cords, 'Quipu del Mani' nickname, Sector A UE 11.
  - 1-PAIRE architecture with L3 depth.
  - Commodity-accounting classification (now sharpened to tier-2 regional aggregator).
narrative:
UR269 opens with nine empty cords. Nine blank strings of cotton, no knots, no colour meaningful -- a physical pause the scribe built into the document before the work begins. No other Incahuasi khipu in the analysed set does this. Perhaps it is a header convention. Perhaps it is the physical space where the document rolled up for storage. Either way, the reader approaches UR269 through a blank opening.

Then the work begins, and it is the same work, repeated two hundred times. At cord seventy, the scribe writes on white: two thousand seven hundred. The gross deposit of one peanut consignment, arrived from some locality in the Canete valley. At cord seventy-one, on near-black: two thousand six hundred. The net after tax. The difference -- one hundred -- is what Inca administration takes off the top before the remainder enters the Qollqa. At cord seventy-three, white: two thousand eight hundred and ten. At seventy-four, near-black: two thousand seven hundred. Difference one hundred and ten. At eighty-three, white: two thousand four hundred and sixty. At eighty-four, near-black: two thousand four hundred and eighteen. Difference forty-two. The tax is not a flat constant here -- it varies by deposit, a negotiated or assessed figure for each locality. This is why UR269 is the TIER-2 AGGREGATOR: the district-level constants (forty-seven for aji, two hundred and eight for peanut) emerge from the many local variable taxes reconciled here, one cord-pair at a time.

For over two hundred cord-pairs this rhythm continues. Gross on white, net on near-black, the difference silently readable between them as a tax-per-deposit. Every fifteen to twenty cords, a vocabulary word rises from the numbers to index the provenance: piqa at cord eighty for the responsible kuraka, taqa at a hundred and six for the boundary, qaqa at a hundred and forty for the landmark rock, waqa at a hundred and eighty for the huaca witness, chaqaqa at a hundred and ninety for the main bridge, paqa at a hundred and forty-one for the east. Nine words across three hundred and thirty cords, none frequent, all structural: they name WHERE each cluster of deposits came from in the Canete valley.

The Ascher lattice is the tallest of the Incahuasi set -- four levels of Merkle cascade -- but not one of the seventeen declared sums verifies under modern parsing. This is the same issue UR270 and UR277 have: the OKR transcription is missing S-knot structural information that KFG's direct measurements show. The arithmetic is CORRECT; the data-capture discrepancy prevents modern machine parsing from closing it. The Benford match drops to zero point fifty-seven, a value characteristic of quota-round numbers (two thousands, two thousand threes, two thousand fives, two thousand sevens) rather than raw tallies -- exactly what one would expect from an aggregator whose inputs have been rounded to standard quota classes.

The document closes with two long empty blocks: fourteen empties from cord two hundred and fifty-one to two hundred and sixty-four, thirteen more from two hundred and seventy-seven to two hundred and eighty-nine. Twenty-seven empty cords at the end, more than any other Incahuasi khipu. Either reserved capacity for future cycles, or ritual closure, or physical spacers for the rolling-up of the document. UR269 closes in silence, the same way it opened.

The document is the SECOND Quipu del Mani -- the first being UR270 at UE 13, the liturgical day-book. This is the bookkeeper's workbook: UR270 gathered the peanuts as they arrived; UR269 here reconciled them into quota-compatible aggregates; UR268 at UE 18 took those aggregates and applied the two-hundred-and-eight-constant flat tax to produce the district summary. Three khipus, three storerooms, one complete pipeline, running the peanut economy of Incahuasi for whatever span of years the Inca state needed.

prior_work.claim: The three-tier Incahuasi peanut pipeline (UR270 UE 13 tier-1 day-book -> UR269 UE 11 tier-2 aggregator -> UR268 UE 18 tier-3 district summary) is established in published literature.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. (2017). Inka History in Knots. University of Texas Press, pp. 162-178.

prior_work.claim: The Urton-Chu (2015) tax formula (total = fixed-tax + remainder) applies across the peanut pipeline with the 47 and 208 positional constants as tier-3 signatures.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse. Latin American Antiquity 26(4): 512-529.

prior_work.claim: UR269 is nicknamed 'Quipu del Mani' (second khipu with this nickname, alongside UR270).
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository field-note record for UR269 (Urton).

prior_work.claim: UR269 is cited in Medrano & Khosla (2024) as part of the Incahuasi corpus updates.
prior_work.citation: Medrano, M. & Khosla, J. (2024). Knotted-string research revisions.

v3_contribution.claim: ~200 paired W(gross)/NB(net) cord-pairs directly implement the Urton-Chu tax formula at cord-pair level -- the longest direct demonstration of the formula in the analysed ALBA corpus. Variable per-deposit taxes (diffs 42, 100, 110, 133) confirm UR269 is a REGIONAL AGGREGATOR reconciling local assessments, not applying a flat district constant.

evidence.test: Count of W/NB paired cord-pairs implementing gross-tax=net arithmetic

v3_contribution.claim: DEEPEST Merkle cascade (depth 4) of the analysed Incahuasi set -- structural signature of a TIER-2 AGGREGATOR with hierarchical audit trail feeding upward.

evidence.test: Max cascade depth of Ascher sum dependencies

v3_contribution.claim: 9-cord EMPTY opening (p1-p9) is UNIQUE in the analysed Incahuasi set -- a header-convention or physical-spacer signature that distinguishes UR269 from all siblings.

evidence.test: Consecutive EMPTY cord count at document opening

v3_contribution.claim: Benford match 0.57 is CHARACTERISTIC of standardized quota values rather than raw tallies, confirming the aggregator role: UR270 (tier-1 raw) scores 0.83, UR269 (tier-2 aggregator) scores 0.57 -- the first-digit distribution is rounded.

evidence.test: Benford first-digit distribution match score comparison across pipeline tiers

v3_contribution.claim: v3 merged corpus unlocks 21 STRING readings from v1's 2. Nine-word provenance-index vocabulary: piqa / taqa / chaqa / qaqa / waqa / chaqaqa / chiqa / maqa / paqa -- indexes WHICH localities / landmarks / huacas / bridges supplied each aggregated deposit.

evidence.test: Dictionary-confirmed STRING readings after KFG merge

companions.note: Tier-1 warehouse day-book ('Quipu del Mani', UE 13). Shares the nickname with UR269; UR269 aggregates UR270's raw counts into quota-compatible aggregates via the paired W/NB tax formula.
Tier-3 district summary (UE 18, 208-constant, presentation binding). Receives UR269's tier-2 aggregates and applies the flat 208 positional tax for the district-level total.
Audit-grade peanut cadastral survey at UE 19 (47-constant, 5/5 Ascher verified). UR275 complements the three-tier pipeline by providing parcel-level measurements.

Aji tier-1 day-book at Sector A UE 16 (15-constant). Structural parallel: the aji pipeline is COMPACTED into a 2-document pair (UR267A+UR267B) rather than a 3-UE pipeline like the peanut (UR270+UR269+UR268).
UE 16 commodity production calendar. Urton's trilogy of the Incahuasi storehouse: UR270 count, UR278 calendar, UR277 map. UR269 is the aggregator workbook feeding the pipeline.
UE 17 cadastral survey. The MAP of the Incahuasi complex. UR269's provenance-index vocabulary (piqa/qaqa/taqa/chaqa/waqa) references the same landscape UR277 surveys.

word_reading.note: 21/21 dictionary-confirmed STRING readings (100 percent hit rate after KFG merge). Nine-word provenance-index vocabulary. No Pass 3A/3D needed.

document_type.note: Commodity_accounting tier-2 aggregator anchored by (a) 'Quipu del Mani' nickname, (b) 3-tier pipeline position (Urton 2017), (c) ~200 paired W/NB cord-pairs implementing the Urton-Chu tax formula, (d) deepest Merkle cascade (depth 4), (e) Benford 0.57 characteristic of quota values. Strong convergent evidence.

narrative.note: Internally coherent (9-empty opening -> 200 paired W/NB deposits -> provenance-index vocabulary every 10-20 cords -> 27 closing empties). Externally corroborated by Urton 2017 on the Incahuasi three-tier pipeline and by Benford's law on quota aggregation.

data_integrity.note: 0/17 Ascher verified is due to OKR S-knot transcription issue (same as UR270/UR277), not arithmetic failure. KFG declares 43 sum relations across all fieldmark types. covering_ratio 0.16 LOW. The textual channel (syllabary) and the paired W/NB formula channel are trustworthy; the audit-lattice is de-facto unverifiable from current OKR parse.

sections.title: Nine-Empty Opening
sections.reading: UNIQUE to UR269: 9 consecutive EMPTY cords opening the document. Header convention or physical spacer -- no other Incahuasi khipu in the analysed set exhibits this.

key_cords.note: FIRST of 9 consecutive empty cords -- unique opening signature

sections.title: Opening Compound Records
sections.reading: Heavy L1 cords at p14, p18, p19, p23, p24 carrying 2 subsidiaries each. First set of deposits being processed in the aggregator, with depth-L2 sub-classifications.

key_cords.note: heavy L1 with 2 L2 subsidiaries -- opening compound record

sections.title: Paired W/NB Body (Tax Formula)
sections.reading: The TAX FORMULA BODY: ~200 cord-pairs implementing (gross - per-deposit-tax = net) across the full document. Vocabulary indexes provenance every 10-20 cords: piqa (responsible kuraka), taqa (boundary), qaqa (rock), waqa (huaca), chaqa (bridge), paqa (east), chiqa (verification), maqa (strike). The aggregator's core work.

key_cords.note: net 2600 -- tax of 100 on this deposit
TOPIC-MARKED main bridge -- central provenance anchor

sections.title: First Empty Block
sections.reading: 14 consecutive EMPTY cords. Reserved capacity, ritual closure, or physical spacer before the terminal summary.

key_cords.note: first of 14 consecutive empties -- closing begins

sections.title: Terminal Summary
sections.reading: Brief summary block between the two empty closures.

sections.title: Second Empty Block
sections.reading: 13 more consecutive EMPTY cords. Final document closure -- 27 empties combined with Block IV, the longest closing empty-cord signature in the Incahuasi set.

key_cords.note: first of 13 consecutive empties -- terminal closure
last empty cord -- document formally closes
